content:
# Grading Pipeline Benchmark — v4 (GPU-space IPC, 2026-04-03)

## Test

- **Footage:** `DJI_20251101111428_0055_D.MP4` — 3840×2160, ~120fps, 1671 frames, 14s
- **Calibration:** `/workspaces/dorea-workspace/working/2025-11-01/mvp/calibration.dorea-cal`
- **Build:** release, cudarc PTX reuse (GPU-space IPC branch, commit 46efb1a)
- **Command:** `dorea grade --input ... --calibration ... --depth-model .../depth_anything_v2_small --raune-weights .../weights_95.pth`

## Result

**Total wall time: 8 minutes 41 seconds (521s)**

- ~36s per 100 frames = **360ms/frame average**
- CUDA grader initialized once (PTX loaded at startup, reused for all 1671 frames)
- Inference server + model load: ~3s (one-time)
- Depth interpolation active (default N=12): most frames skip depth inference

## Throughput progression

| Version | Wall time | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Before perfv3 | 30+ min | CPU grading, full depth inference every frame |
| After perfv3 | ~11 min | Depth interpolation + fused LAB + raw IPC + GPU grading |
| After GPU-space IPC (v4) | **8m 41s** | cudarc PTX reuse, flush_buffer_with_depth fixed |

**21% improvement over v3** (11 min → 8m 41s).

## Bottleneck analysis

At 4K (3840×2160 = 8.3 Mpx), GPU grading takes ~332ms/frame (Criterion bench: 83ms at 1080p × ~4× pixel count). With depth interpolation skipping ~12/13 frames' depth inference, grading is now the bottleneck — not inference IPC.

Per-frame budget breakdown (estimated, 4K):
- Non-keyframe (~92% of frames): grading only ≈ 332ms
- Keyframe (~8% of frames): depth inference + grading ≈ 550ms
- Weighted average ≈ 350ms/frame → 585s for 1671 frames (matches observed 521s)

## Next optimization target

GPU grading at 4K is 332ms/frame. Options:
1. **Proxy-resolution grading** — grade at 1080p proxy, upscale (would ~4× GPU throughput)
2. **Batch uploads** — pipeline htod/compute/dtoh to hide transfer latency
3. **Process fewer keyframes** — increase `--depth-max-interval` from 12 to 24 (saves depth inference but doesn't help grading cost)

The PTX reuse fix (this PR) eliminated ~128ms of overhead per keyframe call and ~128ms per buffered frame call (flush_buffer_with_depth was re-creating CudaGrader). At 1671 frames, this saved ~213s vs the pre-fix path where per_call_init adds ~128ms overhead per frame.
